segments.text: O Lord, I call to you; hasten to me! Give ear to my voice when I call to you!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let my prayer be counted as incense before you, and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Set a guard, O Lord, over my mouth; keep watch over the door of my lips!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Do not let my heart incline to any evil, to busy myself with wicked deeds in wickedness with men who work iniquity, and let me not eat of their delicacies!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let a righteous man strike me—it is a kindness; let him rebuke me—it is oil for my head; let my head not refuse it. Yet my prayer is continually against their evil deeds.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: me; it shall be a: or, me kindly, and reprove me; let not their precious oil break, etc

segments.text: When their judges are thrown down the sides of the cliff, then they shall hear my words, for they are pleasant.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: As when one plows and breaks up the earth, so shall our bones be scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But my eyes are toward you, O God, my Lord; in you I seek refuge; leave me not defenseless!
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: leave: Heb. make not my soul bare

segments.text: Keep me from the trap that they have laid for me and from the snares of evildoers!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, while I escape.
segments.speaker: David
